# memory/context/

Per-topic context reconstructions. "What was the state of this topic when I last worked on it?"

**What goes here:** Compressed context assembled from all memory layers for a specific topic. What's decided, what's open, what to read first, what to do next. Generated by /mem-reconstruct at the END of a work session.

**What does NOT go here:** Live notes (use notes/raw/), workflow state (use P15 runtime/), raw context without synthesis.

**When to create:** At the end of any deep work session on a topic that will be revisited in >7 days.

**Update rule:** Regenerate rather than update. Stale reconstructions are misleading. Archive the old file before creating the new one.

**Skill:** `/mem-reconstruct`
**Schema:** `architecture/MEMORY-SCHEMAS.md` → Schema 7
**File naming:** `ctx_SLUG.md` (one per topic, regenerated — not appended)
**Review interval:** 30 days from `as_of`
**Archive trigger:** Topic complete, or reconstruction is > 30 days old on an active topic (needs regeneration)
**Completion:** When a topic is done, write a final reconstruction with `status: complete` and archive
